title: Alfonso Luiz Obota
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Obata.jpg]] |name=Alfonso Luiz Obota |homeworld=[[Adega system/Legends|Adega system]]''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners= |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My name is Obota—Alfonso Obota—Al to my friends.|Alfonso Luiz Obota|}} '''Alfonso Luiz Obota''', also known as '''Al''', was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of the [[Adega system/Legends|Adega system]]. A graduate of the [[Merchant Academy]], Obota joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and became the [[Second officer/Legends|Second Officer]] on the [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Alliance Special Operations]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Spirit of Solaris|''Pride of Aridus'']]. During an [[Infiltration of unidentified Imperial planet|infiltration mission]] on an [[Unidentified planet (Alliance Special Ops operation)|Imperial planet]], a Special Ops team fell under heavy fire from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces, and the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Nord (Rebel)|Nord]], wished to leave them behind. Obota did not agree to do so and led a mutiny against Nord, confining him in his cabin and taking control of the ship. Obota was able to extract three SpecOps members, but during the escape from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], five crew members were lost when [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] attacked the ''Pride of Aridus''. For his rebellious actions, Obota was court-martialed and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] in [[Military Correction Facility 63]] on the planet [[Milagro/Legends|Milagro]], where he spent several months. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], he was visited by two Rebel agents, [[Jan Ors]] and [[Kyle Katarn]]. The two secured Obota's release so he could help them land on the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], which was under the control of the Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec's]] forces. Obota was to impersonate the Imperial [[Deck officer/Legends|deck officer]] [[Alfonso Luiz Obota|Hortu Agar]] in charge of the transport ''[[High Hauler]]'', which was carrying a cargo of defective [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] that would attract the Imperials' attention. Having reached Ruusan, Obota gave the torpedoes to Jerec's [[Troop/Legends|troops]], and soon the ''High Hauler'' blended with the rest of the force. As members of the transport's crew, Ors and Katarn were then able to land on Ruusan in their ship, the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''. ==Biography== The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Alfonso Luiz Obota —referred to as "Al" by his friends— was a native of the [[Adega system/Legends|Adega system]] who studied at the [[Merchant Academy]] and graduated fourth in his class. By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' states Obota's service as third officer occurred a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the events of the novel. ''Jedi Knight'' is dated to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. he qualified as third officer on a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] but soon resigned and joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. ===Mutiny and imprisonment=== {{Quote|Three lives for five... a rather poor trade, wouldn't you say?|Jan Ors, to Alfonso Luiz Obota|}} Obota was commissioned as [[Second Lieutenant/Legends|second lieutenant]] and became the [[Second officer/Legends|Second Officer]] on the [[Spirit of Solaris|''Pride of Aridus'']], an [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Alliance Special Operations]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Nord (Rebel)|Nord]]. Approximately six [[standard month/Legends|months]] after Obota had been commissioned, the ''Pride of Aridus'' participated in a [[Infiltration of unidentified Imperial planet|Special Ops operation]] on an [[Unidentified planet (Alliance Special Ops operation)|Imperial-controlled planet]]. Under the guise of discharging a legitimate cargo, the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] landed a twelve-man team of [[Commando/Legends|commandos]], who —according to the insertion plan— were supposed to come back in exactly six [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]. The group however was late by two [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. Nord was almost ready to leave without them when the commandos finally raced toward the ship under heavy fire from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]]. As the ''Pride of Aridus'' fell under fire as well, Nord gave the order to take off, intent on leaving the SpecOps team behind. Not willing to abandon his fellow Rebels, Obota punched Nord in the face and confined him in his cabin. Obota then seized control of the ship and waited for the commandos, three of whom were able to reach the ship and were successfully extracted thanks to Obota's actions. However, when the ''Pride of Aridus'' finally lifted off and reached the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], it was attacked by several [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]]. In the ensuing battle, five crew members were [[Death/Legends|killed]], and the ship suffered serious damage, barely escaping by making a jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. For his mutiny, Obota was court-marshaled -Nord claimed that Obota had always been insabordinate- and sentenced to confinement. Obota was given the serial number "'''Prisoner 272-20-136'''" and spent the following six months working and contemplating his past actions. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' is dated to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. he served in the [[Military Correction Facility 63]] on the planet [[Milagro/Legends|Milagro]], part of a recently constructed New Republic base, assigned to digging work among the ruins of a city devastated by the Empire, under the watchful [[Eye/Legends|eye]] of [[detainment droid]]s. While he was drilling a hole on the slag, intended to reach three [[Meter/Legends|meters]] deep into the planet's crust. His work was interrupted, ordered to report to the headquarters. Despite ordered to hurry, he had to wait standing for ten minutes in an office, until he was visited by two Rebel agents—[[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jan Ors]]—who requested an audience with Obota. The two questioned him about the SpecOps operation and asked if, knowing the consequences of his actions, he would have made the same decision again. Without a second thought, Obota answered that he would, and his answer satisfied Ors. She proceeded to offer Obota a pardon and a reinstatement as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], in exchange for his services as a [[Deck officer/Legends|deck officer]] during a secret Rebel mission: the [[Mission to Ruusan|infiltration]] of the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], which was controlled by the forces of the Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]. The Inquisitor was intent on harnessing the power of the planet's [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] located in the [[Valley of the Jedi]], and Katarn wanted to prevent him from doing so. Believing that the mission could not be worse than his [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] on Milagro, Obota accepted the offer.[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]] ===Scam=== {{Dialogue|Katarn|A sterling performance.|Ors|Couldn't have been better. You missed a career on the stage.|Obota|Thank you. But that was little more than the first act. The second act is about to begin, and the audience is on its way.|attr=Kyle Katarn, Jan Ors and Alfonso Luiz Obota|src=}} Jerec's forces had established a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] around Ruusan and did not allow anyone near the planet without required recognition codes, which were unknown to Katarn and Ors. The two, however, formulated a plan to infiltrate Ruusan as part of the crew of the Imperial transport ''[[High Hauler]]'', which would be carrying a cargo of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]]. Katarn and Ors hoped that Jerec's [[Troop/Legends|troops]] would not miss a chance to receive additional ordnance and would permit the transport to reach Ruusan without the recognition codes. To explain the absence of those codes and the transport's appearance near the planet, a fake story was formulated, according to which, the ''High Hauler'' had been carrying the torpedoes to the planet [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], but had been caught in a battle with the Rebels, had suffered heavy damage, and had escaped to Ruusan. During the "battle," the ship's [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], Captain [[Drax]], and the majority of the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] crew had been killed, and the [[Engineering officer/Legends|engineering officer]], Lieutenant [[Alfonso Luiz Obota|Hortu Agar]]—whom Obota was to portray—assumed command. Since recognition codes were issued on a need-to-know basis and engineering officers were not cleared to receive them, that would have explained their absence to the Imperials. [[File:Youhavebeentricked.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Obota, disguised as Hortu Agar]] To accommodate this story, the entire ship was given a battle-weary look, complete with makeshift patches, dangling cables, and fire-blackened control consoles. Reflecting "heavy casualties," the transport was crewed with only twelve men out of the intended twenty-five. The crew members, including Obota, wore [[Blood/Legends|blood]]-stained bandages and carefully applied makeup. After running more than twenty simulations, the transport embarked on the actual mission. Having reached Ruusan, the crew of the ''High Hauler'' witnessed a large [[Vengeance Battle Group|battle group]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the planet. The battle group consisted of three [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]], about two dozen [[Picket ship/Legends|picket ships]], and Jerec's personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]. Although he had been expecting to see something similar to this, Obota was still frightened by the size of the enemy force. Nevertheless, when the ''Vengeance'' hailed the ''High Hauler'', Obota answered the call and played his part, telling the Imperials the fabricated story. Although the Imperials initially wanted to destroy the ''High Hauler'', the mention of the proton torpedoes forced them to change their mind. They dispatched a boarding party to inspect the ship's condition and secure the torpedoes, which, in fact, were defective and would explode in the launch tube if used. After the boarding party, led by an [[Unidentified Imperial lieutenant (Ruusan)|Imperial lieutenant]], finished its cursory inspection and left, a [[lighter]] arrived and took all the torpedoes. After that, Obota signaled to Jerec's troops that he was going to make necessary repairs to his vessel and dispatched Katarn and Ors aboard their ship—the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''—on a series of errands to the surface of Ruusan. Those errands included the delivery of spare parts, the securing of food rations, and a fictional training "mission" that allowed the two agents to pass through Ruusan's heavily guarded northern hemisphere. In fact, the objective of such missions was to help Katarn and Ors familiarize themselves with the Imperial operation and establish the ''Moldy Crow'' within the pattern of the battle group's comings and goings. Sixteen hours later, the ''High Hauler'' was ready to leave and received all necessary clearances to do so. While preparing for a jump to hyperspace, Obota steered the ship toward a restricted Class I security zone at Ruusan's atmosphere, where the ''Moldy Crow'', with Katarn and Ors on board, left the transport's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and set off for the planet's surface. Although Obota's maneuver had been noticed by the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Unidentified Imperial fleet operations officer|operations officer]], he did not report it and wrote it off to the commanding officer's lack of experience. Eventually, Katarn was able to reach the Valley of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], where he confronted and killed Jerec. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|So, knowing how the whole thing turned out, would you make the same decision again?''"
"''Ma'am! Yes, ma'am!''"
"''Why?''"
"''Because it seemed like the right thing to do.|Alfonso Luiz Obota and Jan Ors|}} Obota was an experienced deck officer and was caring toward his fellow Rebels. His unwillingness to abandon the Special Ops team led him to start a mutiny, although Captain Nord implied that Obota showed a fair amount of disobedience since the beginning of his service aboard the ''Pride of Aridus''. Obota fully realized the results of his decision and spent countless days and nights on Milagro thinking about it. He remembered the faces of the five crew members who had died and wished that he had died instead of them. Overall, however, he did not regret his actions and thought that he would have done the same thing even if he had known the outcome. Even on Milagro, Obota still displayed defiance by taking an unauthorized break from his work. When a detainment [[Droid/Legends|droid]] noticed this and gave him a warning, Obota behaved sarcastically toward the machine. He also did not like to be addressed as "Prisoner 272-20-136" and unsuccessfully tried to make the droid call him by his real name. After running many simulations of the Ruusan operation, Obota expected to find an Imperial battle group above the planet but was nevertheless scared by the sight of it. During the inspection, he could not help but like the lieutenant in charge of the boarding party, who was very friendly and talkative. Still, Obota listened carefully to everything he said, as he realized that every bit of information could be important. He was cautious and did not allow the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] of the lighter to step aboard the ''High Hauler''. He did so by leaving the hangar open to [[Realspace/Legends|space]], thus forcing the Empire to rely on droids during the loading of the torpedoes. Obota had some acting talent, which allowed him to play the role of Hortu Agar. He had dark brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]] a [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoo]] on his right shoulder. After the Imperials finished loading the torpedoes from the ''High Hauler'', Obota wished to remain near Ruusan long enough to see the damage the defective ordnance would cause, but he complied with Ors' order to leave the planet as soon as he received all clearances. ==Behind the scenes== Alfonso Luiz Obota first appeared in ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'', a [[1998]] novella written by [[William C. Dietz]]. He later appeared in the [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] of the novel, voiced by [[Emil Herrera]].''[[Dark Forces: The Collector's Trilogy]]'' audio drama'' ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' {{1st}} *[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obota, Alfonso Luiz}} [[Category:Alliance Military officers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Impersonators]] [[Category:Males]]
